creator: b.ecker@bitteeinbyte.de
text:
Thank you for the swift reply.
Here are the commands and subsequent outputs you asked for.


mt -f /dev/sa0 status -v
Drive: sa0: <HP Ultrium 3-SCSI G54D> Serial Number: HU107036G0
---------------------------------
Mode      Density              Blocksize      bpi      Compression
Current:  0x44:LTO-3           variable       244805   enabled (0x1)
---------------------------------
Current Driver State: at rest.
---------------------------------
Partition:   0      Calc File Number:   0     Calc Record Number: 0
Residual:    0  Reported File Number:   0 Reported Record Number: 0
Flags: BOP
---------------------------------
Tape I/O parameters:
  Maximum I/O size allowed by driver and controller (maxio): 1048576 bytes
  Maximum I/O size reported by controller (cpi_maxio): 1048576 bytes
  Maximum block size supported by tape drive and media (max_blk): 16777215 bytes
  Minimum block size supported by tape drive and media (min_blk): 1 bytes
  Block granularity supported by tape drive and media (blk_gran): 0 bytes
  Maximum possible I/O size (max_effective_iosize): 1048576 bytes


camcontrol attrib sa0 -v -r attr_val | head -n2
Remaining Capacity in Partition (0x0000)[8](RO): 399915 MB
Maximum Capacity in Partition (0x0001)[8](RO): 400308 MB


dd if=/dev/urandom of=/dev/nsa0 bs=1048576
dd: /dev/nsa0: end of device
881+0 records in
880+0 records out
922746880 bytes transferred in 16.470389 secs (56024594 bytes/sec)


mt errstat
Last I/O Residual: 0
 Last I/O Command: 0A 00 10 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
   Last I/O Sense:

         00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
         00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00

Last Control Residual: 0
 Last Control Command: 34 06 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
   Last Control Sense:

         00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
         00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00


mt -f /dev/nsa0 status -v
Drive: sa0: <HP Ultrium 3-SCSI G54D> Serial Number: HU107036G0
---------------------------------
Mode      Density              Blocksize      bpi      Compression
Current:  0x44:LTO-3           variable       244805   enabled (0x1)
---------------------------------

---------------------------------
Partition:   0      Calc File Number:   0     Calc Record Number: 0
Residual:    0  Reported File Number:   0 Reported Record Number: 880
Flags: None

Tape I/O parameters:
  Maximum I/O size allowed by driver and controller (maxio): 1048576 bytes
  Maximum I/O size reported by controller (cpi_maxio): 1048576 bytes
  Maximum block size supported by tape drive and media (max_blk): 16777215 bytes
  Minimum block size supported by tape drive and media (min_blk): 1 bytes
  Block granularity supported by tape drive and media (blk_gran): 0 bytes
  Maximum possible I/O size (max_effective_iosize): 1048576 bytes


I then rewound the tape and ran the same procedure again, which yields similar but not identical results.
mt -f /dev/nsa0 rewind; dd if=/dev/urandom of=/dev/nsa0 bs=1048576; mt -f /dev/nsa0 errstat; mt -f /dev/nsa0 status -v
dd: /dev/nsa0: end of device
926+0 records in
925+0 records out
969932800 bytes transferred in 13.731777 secs (70634181 bytes/sec)
Last I/O Residual: 0
 Last I/O Command: 0A 00 10 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
   Last I/O Sense:

         00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
         00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00

Last Control Residual: 0

         00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
         00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00

---------------------------------

---------------------------------

Residual:    0  Reported File Number:   0 Reported Record Number: 925
Flags: None
---------------------------------

  Maximum I/O size reported by controller (cpi_maxio): 1048576 bytes
  Maximum block size supported by tape drive and media (max_blk): 16777215 bytes
  Minimum block size supported by tape drive and media (min_blk): 1 bytes

I hope this helps. Thank you for helping.
